channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: 'BusySnake' Infostealer Slithers into Critical Infrastructure Networks
item.description: A threat group researchers call "Armored Likho" has gained access to government agencies and electrical power entities in Russia, Brazil, and Kazakhstan....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CitrixBleed-ing Again? NetScaler Vulnerability Under Attack
item.description: Attackers wasted little time targeting the latest memory disclosure flaw in Citrix's NetScaler products, after researchers published a proof-of-concept exploit (PoC)....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Phishing poses as big-brand job interview to steal Google accounts
item.description: A phishing campaign is impersonating more than 30 well-known brands, including Adobe, Netflix, Coca-Cola, and OpenAI, in fake job interviews to steal Google account credentials from marketing professi...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake IT support calls on Microsoft Teams push EtherRAT malware
item.description: Threat actors are abusing Microsoft Teams voice calls by impersonating corporate IT support staff to trick employees into installing the EtherRAT malware, giving attackers initial access to corporate ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Use New Cavern C2 Framework to Target Israeli Organizations
item.description: An Iranian hacking group affiliated with Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS) has been wielding a previously undocumented modular command-and-control (C2) framework dubbed Cavern (aka C...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Vietnam arrests suspects behind HiAnime anime piracy service
item.description: ​Vietnamese authorities have arrested and are prosecuting seven suspects believed to have run HiAnime, the largest anime piracy streaming service before its shutdown in June. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 16-Year-Old Linux KVM Flaw Lets Guest VMs Escape to Host on Intel and AMD x86 Systems
item.description:
A use-after-free bug in Linux's KVM hypervisor can be triggered from a guest virtual machine to corrupt the shadow-page state of the host kernel that runs it.
Dubbed 'Januscape' and tracked as CVE-20...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: JadePuffer: The First Complete LLM-Driven Ransomware Attack
item.description: An "agentic threat actor" successfully exploited a Langflow flaw to steal data from a production database server and encrypt other systems....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Threat Actors Probe Gitea Docker Flaw CVE-2026-20896 13 Days After Disclosure
item.description:
Threat actors have been observed attempting to exploit a recently patched critical security flaw in Gitea Docker images, according to Sysdig.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-20896 (CVSS sco...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Software Is Now Written at the Speed of Thought. Security Isn't.
item.description: Every evolution in software development has reduced the friction between an idea and a deployable application. AI may remove the final barrier, but it also removes many of the moments where security d...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Max severity Adobe ColdFusion flaw now exploited in attacks
item.description: Attackers are now exploiting a maximum-severity Adobe ColdFusion vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-48282, according to vulnerability intelligence company KEVIntel. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Proxy Botnets, Browser Ransomware, AI Agent Tricks, Fake PoC Malware and More
item.description: A streaming box should not need a threat model. Neither should a username field, a demo repo, a reset flow, or a browser permission prompt. That is the irritating part this week: the risky pieces were...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: How to Evaluate an AI SOC Platform in 2026: 6 Capabilities That Separate Leaders from Bolt-On AI solutions
item.description: Building a shortlist for an AI SOC evaluation can be tough. SIEM, SOAR, and pureplay AI SOC vendors are all saying the same thing. But behind the identical label sit very different products, from chat...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Suspected China-Nexus Hackers Use Fake Indian Tax Filing Utility to Deploy DcRAT
item.description: A suspected China-nexus threat activity cluster has been observed targeting Indian taxpayers, tax professionals, and corporate finance teams to deliver a remote access trojan designed to steal sensiti...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New TrojPix Attack Leaks Data From Air-Gapped Systems via Video Cable Emissions
item.description: Researchers at Shandong University have shown a fast new way to pull data off computers that are cut off from every network. The technique, called TrojPix, tweaks on-screen pixels in ways the eye cann...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Java-Based QuimaRAT MaaS Built to Run on Windows, Linux, and macOS
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a novel Java-based remote access trojan (RAT) called QuimaRAT that's capable of targeting Windows, Linux, and macOS environments.
According to LevelBlue, the cr...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Opera GX Flaw Let Malicious Sites Auto-Install Mods to Steal Data From Visited Pages
item.description: Researchers found a flaw in Opera GX, the gaming-focused version of the Opera browser, that let a malicious website silently install a browser add-on and use it to lift specific data from the pages a ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: SkillCloak Lets Malicious AI Agent Skills Evade Static Scanners with Self-Extracting Packing
item.description: Scanners meant to catch malicious add-on "skills" for AI coding agents can be fooled by a few simple changes that leave the malware working, according to a new study from researchers at the Hong Kong ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Flipper Zero firmware development continues with community help
item.description: Flipper Devices says development of the Flipper Zero firmware will continue, albeit with a smaller internal team and greater reliance on community contributions. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: JadePuffer ransomware used AI agent to automate entire attack
item.description: Researchers identified what they believe is the first documented case of a ransomware operation, JadePuffer, conducted entirely by a large language model (LLM) agent. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: U.S. Government Entity Paid Kairos $1 Million in Data-Theft Extortion Case
item.description: A U.S. government entity paid about $1 million to keep stolen files from being leaked, according to a new case study by Rakesh Krishnan for Ransom-ISAC, built on a leaked negotiation chat and the bloc...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: North Korean Hackers Publish 108 Malicious Packages and Extensions in PolinRider Campaign
item.description: The North Korean threat actors linked to the Contagious Interview campaign have been observed publishing 108 unique packages and web browser extensions spanning npm, Packagist, Go, and Google Chrome a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Unpatched Flaws Disclosed in Filesystem Bundled Into Millions of Embedded Devices
item.description:
Security firm runZero has disclosed seven vulnerabilities in FatFs, a small filesystem library that lets a device read and write the FAT and exFAT formats used on USB drives and SD cards.
The flaws m...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New "Bad Epoll" Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Unprivileged Users Gain Root, Hits Android
item.description: A newly disclosed Linux kernel flaw called Bad Epoll (CVE-2026-46242) lets an ordinary user with no special access take full control of a machine as root. It affects Linux desktops, servers, and Andro...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Avalon Malware Framework Packs CrownX Ransomware Capabilities
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented modular malware framework codenamed Avalon that's distributed by means of a multi-stage phishing chain capable of bypassing traditio...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: NetNut proxy network disrupted, 2 million infected devices cut off
item.description: A joint operation involving Google has disrupted NetNut, a residential proxy network that gave access to millions of compromised Android devices, including smart TVs and streaming boxes. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: North Korea-Linked npm Packages Mimic Rollup Polyfills to Steal Developer Secrets
item.description:
Threat actors with ties to North Korea have been linked to a fresh set of malicious npm packages that masquerade as Rollup polyfill tooling to facilitate remote access and data theft.
According to JF...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ARToken PhaaS exposes EvilTokens' Microsoft 365 phishing toolkit
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform dubbed "ARToken" appears to operate as an affiliate of the EvilTokens phishing platform, giving researchers a glimpse into an extensive toolkit designed to...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Armored Likho Targets Government Agencies, Power Sector with BusySnake Stealer
item.description:
A previously undocumented threat actor known as Armored Likho has been attributed to cyber attacks targeting government agencies and the electric power sector across Russia, Brazil, and Kazakhstan.
"...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Chinese LLMs Broaden the Gap Between Attackers & Defenders
item.description: Two new models from Chinese firms compete with top US mainstream and frontier models. Should cyber-defenders be worried?...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
